outputs.name: Arguments
outputs.tooltip: The raw argument string passed after the command name.

inputs.name: AnchorX
inputs.tooltip: Horizontal screen anchor as a percentage (0 = left, 0.5 = center, 1 = right).

inputs.name: AnchorY
inputs.tooltip: Vertical screen anchor as a percentage (0 = top, 0.5 = center, 1 = bottom).

inputs.name: Angle
inputs.tooltip: Rotation angle in degrees.

inputs.name: FontColor
inputs.tooltip: Text color.

inputs.name: Lifetime
inputs.tooltip: How long the text remains visible in seconds. 0 for infinite.

inputs.name: OutlineColor
inputs.tooltip: Font outline color.

inputs.name: OutlineSize
inputs.tooltip: Font outline thickness in pixels. 0 disables the outline.

inputs.name: PositionX
inputs.tooltip: Horizontal offset from the anchor in slate units.

inputs.name: PositionY
inputs.tooltip: Vertical offset from the anchor in slate units.

inputs.name: ScaleX
inputs.tooltip: Horizontal scale factor.

inputs.name: ScaleY
inputs.tooltip: Vertical scale factor.

inputs.name: Text
inputs.tooltip: Rich text content to display. Supports markup like <b>, <i>, <size="24">, <color="FF0000">, <emoji>, and <icon>.

inputs.name: TextId
inputs.tooltip: Override the display text identity. When non-zero, multiple gates sharing the same ID control one piece of text. 0 uses the brick's persistent handle. Connect the Text ID output of another Display Text gate to chain updates to the same text.

inputs.name: Transition
inputs.tooltip: Duration in seconds to interpolate from the previous state to the new state. 0 for instant.

inputs.name: Blend
inputs.tooltip: The alpha value for blending between Input A and Input B. 0 means only Input A, 1 means only Input B. Anything between is a mixture of the two - for example, 0.5 would give you a value half way between A and B.

inputs.name: bCollisionEnabled_Player
inputs.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 1.

inputs.name: bCollisionEnabled_Player1
inputs.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 2.

inputs.name: bCollisionEnabled_Player2
inputs.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 3.

inputs.name: bCollisionEnabled_Player3
inputs.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 4.

inputs.name: bCollisionEnabled_Player
inputs.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 1.

inputs.name: bCollisionEnabled_Player1
inputs.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 2.

inputs.name: bCollisionEnabled_Player2
inputs.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 3.

inputs.name: bCollisionEnabled_Player3
inputs.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 4.

inputs.name: ZeroSecondsToWait
inputs.tooltip: How long to hold the output after the input goes to zero. Set to -1 to use Seconds to Delay instead.

inputs.name: ZeroTicksToWait
inputs.tooltip: How long to hold the output after the input goes to zero. Set to -1 to use Ticks to Delay instead.

entries.brickSummary: A brick that can split a Player Input wire from a seat into individual inputs, allowing custom control setups.

inputs.name: Character
inputs.tooltip: Connect a Player reference to allow people to send inputs to this component.

outputs.name: bPressedJump
outputs.tooltip: Set to true if the player is pressing their jump key.

outputs.name: InputForward
outputs.tooltip: The player's movement input on the X axis.

outputs.name: InputRight
outputs.tooltip: The player's movement input on the Y axis.

entries.brickSummary: Allows defining a variable, which can be modified and read via other Variable gates.
Allows defining an array variable, which can be modified and read via other Array Variable gates.
A brick that can be set to a customized weight
A slimmer version of the Wheel Engine. Takes control inputs and uses them to drive & steer vehicle wheels. Great for cars, trucks, tanks, and anything else that drives around on the ground.

inputs.name: AudioDescriptor
inputs.tooltip: The set of sounds the engine uses.

inputs.name: bEnabled
inputs.tooltip: Whether the engine can ever be on.

inputs.name: bEnableManualControl
inputs.tooltip: If true, the engine always stays on as long as Enabled is true. When not driven by a player, it will be controlled by the manual input values.

inputs.name: bManualInput_Brake
inputs.tooltip:
Manual braking mode. True means the engine will brake. 
When a vehicle brakes, the speed of all joints is hard-set to 0 and the torque is set to the braking torque.

inputs.name: bTankSteering
inputs.tooltip:
If true, the engine attempts to steer by driving the wheels on either side of the vehicle at different speeds (i.e. tank controls)
This means the engine won't ever turn the wheels except to correct heading if they are misaligned.

inputs.name: CenterOfSteering
inputs.tooltip:
How far along the vehicle's steering box (box containing all wheels) the center of steering is.
By default, we put it at the very back of the vehicle (0). Wheels at the front will full-steer, wheels at the back won't steer at all.
If you'd like, you can bring it to the very front of the vehicle (1). Wheels at the back will full-steer, wheels at the front won't steer at all.

inputs.name: Character
inputs.tooltip: Connect a Player reference here to allow a player to drive the engine. Seats also provide a Player wire which can be used here.

inputs.name: DriveAcceleratingPowerMultiplier
inputs.tooltip:
Multiplier applied to drive joint torque when accelerating.
If above 1.0, the engine will torque your joint harder than the torque you set.

inputs.name: DriveBrakingPowerMultiplier
inputs.tooltip:
Multiplier applied to drive joint torque when braking.
If above 1.0 (which it probably should be), the engine will torque your joint harder than the torque you set.

inputs.name: DriveDampingMultiplier
inputs.tooltip:
Multiplier applied to drive joint damping, which is used when neither accelerating nor braking (models the vehicle "rolling" to a stop).
If above 1.0, the engine will dampen your joint harder than the damping you set.

inputs.name: DriveInterpSpeed
inputs.tooltip:
Determines how fast the wheel target speed goes from 0 to the top speed. Useful to avoid your car tipping backwards when you accelerate from stopped.
Set 1, and your wheels will not be trying to reach the max speed for a full second.
Set 10, and it will take a tenth of a second.
0 makes it instant, if you enjoy having your car fly upwards when accelerating from stopped.

inputs.name: DriveSpeed
inputs.tooltip:
Determines the max speed at which the engine attempts to drive wheels.
Ignored while braking, obviously.

inputs.name: ManualInput_Drive
inputs.tooltip: Manual amount for the engine to drive. -1 = full reverse, 1 = full accelerate.

inputs.name: ManualInput_Steer
inputs.tooltip: Manual amount for the engine to steer. -1 = full left, 1 = full right.

inputs.name: SteerInterpSpeed
inputs.tooltip:
Determines how fast your wheels steer to their current target.
Set 1, and your wheels will take a full second to steer to the target.
Set 10, and your wheels will take a tenth of a second.
0 makes it instant.

inputs.name: SteerLimitDegree
inputs.tooltip: The maximum in both directions that the engine can steer wheels - i.e. the value in degrees of Steer 1.0 (and if negated, the value of steer -1.0)

inputs.name: SteerPowerMultiplier
inputs.tooltip: Multipler applied to steering power.

inputs.name: TankSteerSpeedMultiplier
inputs.tooltip: Multiplier applied to the drive speed when tank steering.

outputs.name: Control_DriveAndSteer
outputs.tooltip: Connected to wheel joints to allow this engine to drive and steer them.

entries.brickSummary: Allows you to place other logic gates on the microchip's circuitboard to save space. The entire circuitboard collapses down into the microchip when not in use.
A node which wires can be routed through for more direct control over their shape. Useful for organizing cluttered logic circuits.

component.description: This brick can reroute wires.

inputs.name: RER_Input
inputs.tooltip: The input value.

outputs.name: RER_Output
outputs.tooltip: The output value.

entries.brickSummary: Plays a sound when activated by a wire signal.

component.description: Plays a sound when it receives a wire signal.

inputs.name: AudioDescriptor
inputs.tooltip: The audio track to play on this brick.

inputs.name: bEnableRepeat
inputs.tooltip: Toggles whether or not the sound will automatically repeat when the play signal is kept on.

inputs.name: bSpatialization
inputs.tooltip: Whether or not to spatialize the audio in the game world, panning accordingly as the player moves around. When disabled, it will play as a 2D sound.

inputs.name: InnerRadius
inputs.tooltip: The distance before the audio will begin to fade out.

inputs.name: MaxDistance
inputs.tooltip: The distance the audio can no longer be heard.

inputs.name: PitchMultiplier
inputs.tooltip: How much to multiply the default pitch of the audio being played.

inputs.name: RepeatTime
inputs.tooltip: The amount of base time until the sound will repeat again.

inputs.name: RepeatVariance
inputs.tooltip: An applied value of randomness to the base repeat time.

inputs.name: VolumeMultiplier
inputs.tooltip: How much to multiply the default volume of the audio being played.

entries.brickSummary: Plays audio from a wide selection of music tracks and sound effects.

component.description: The brick produces sound continuously.

inputs.name: AudioDescriptor
inputs.tooltip: The audio track to play on this brick.

inputs.name: bEnabled
inputs.tooltip: Whether the sound is enabled.

inputs.name: bSpatialization
inputs.tooltip: Whether or not to spatialize the audio in the game world, panning accordingly as the player moves around. When disabled, it will play as a 2D sound.

inputs.name: FocusAzimuth
inputs.tooltip: Sounds played from within this angle on the player's screen will be considered in focus, and played normally.

inputs.name: InnerRadius
inputs.tooltip: The distance before the audio will begin to fade out.

inputs.name: MaxDistance
inputs.tooltip: The distance the audio can no longer be heard.

inputs.name: NonFocusAzimuth
inputs.tooltip: Sounds above this angle on the player's screen will be considered out of focus, and will have the Unfocused Volume Multiplier applied to it. You can set this value lower than the Focus Angle to flip the effect.

inputs.name: NonFocusVolumeAttenuation
inputs.tooltip: When a sound is out of focus, it will have its volume multiplied by this value.

inputs.name: PitchMultiplier
inputs.tooltip: How much to multiply the default pitch of the audio being played.

inputs.name: VolumeMultiplier
inputs.tooltip: How much to multiply the default volume of the audio being played.

entries.brickSummary: Fires projectiles upon receiving a wire signal.

component.description: Spawns projectiles in a chosen brick-local direction while the Fire wire input is held. Use Fire Rate to control how often the burst repeats while held; 0 fires once per rising edge.

entries.brickSummary: Produces thrust force equivalent to its 'Throttle' input value. Also emits an exhaust plume effect which can be edited or disabled as needed.

component.description: Continuously pushes the brick grid out of the nozzle face. Scale the force with the Throttle wire input and steer the exhaust with Pitch and Yaw.

entries.brickSummary: Plays audio from a wide selection of music tracks and sound effects.

component.description: The brick produces sound continuously.

entries.brickSummary: Outputs a wire signal when hit with a projectile.

inputs.name: OnTime
inputs.tooltip: The amount of time that the target turns "On" when shot. Set to 0 for one tick. The target cannot report new information while it is on.

outputs.name: bJustHit
outputs.tooltip: Whether the target was just hit and is waiting out its hit timeout.

outputs.name: CharacterThatJustHit
outputs.tooltip: Character that just hit the target.

outputs.name: Damage
outputs.tooltip: How much damage came from the hit.

entries.brickSummary: Spawns a bot.

inputs.name: AggroRange
inputs.tooltip: Minimum distance the bot requires to attack a target.

inputs.name: Agression
inputs.tooltip: How much does the bot keep shooting.

inputs.name: AttackMovementAmount
inputs.tooltip: How much does the bot move around while in combat.

inputs.name: bBackVision
inputs.tooltip: Enables 360 degree vision cone

inputs.name: bTeamCollision
inputs.tooltip: Whether a bot  should collide with characters using channel 1.

inputs.name: bTeamCollision1
inputs.tooltip: Whether a bot  should collide with characters using channel 2.

inputs.name: bTeamCollision2
inputs.tooltip: Whether a bot  should collide with characters using channel 3.

inputs.name: bTeamCollision3
inputs.tooltip: Whether a bot  should collide with characters using channel 4.

inputs.name: bVisionRaycasting
inputs.tooltip: Require line of sight to attack someone.

inputs.name: CorpseTimeout
inputs.tooltip: How long we should wait before deleting corpses.

inputs.name: GunSkill
inputs.tooltip: Accuracy of the bot when using guns

inputs.name: HealthMultiplier
inputs.tooltip: Multiplies the amount of health of the bot

inputs.name: Jumpyness
inputs.tooltip: How often does the bot jump

inputs.name: MovementAmount
inputs.tooltip: How often the bot refreshes movement.

inputs.name: MovementRandomness
inputs.tooltip: Random offset applied to movement logic

inputs.name: ReactionTime
inputs.tooltip: How often does the bot recalculate targets.

inputs.name: RespawnTime
inputs.tooltip: How long we should wait before spawning a new bot after the current one dies.

inputs.name: RunSpeed
inputs.tooltip: Multiplier for the walking speed of the bot

inputs.name: TeamCollisionChannel
inputs.tooltip: Which collision channel the bot should use.

outputs.name: DistanceToTarget
outputs.tooltip: Distance to the current movement goal

outputs.name: FocusCharacter
outputs.tooltip: Character this bot is trying to attack

outputs.name: SpawnedCharacter
outputs.tooltip: Reference to the spawned bot

typeGlossary.bool: boolean - port names typically prefixed with b
typeGlossary.int: integer - used by Bitwise family and index args
typeGlossary.float: floating-point scalar - used by Math/Trig and most numeric args
typeGlossary.arrayVarRef: reference to an Array Variable component
typeGlossary.any: polymorphic or unresolved; resolve via PortsAreCompatible or source wire's type
